steps.title: Check the increasing function
steps.content: For $f(x)=16(1.25)^x+43$, the base $1.25$ is greater than $1$. On the domain $x\ge0$, this function increases without bound, so it has no maximum value.

steps.title: Check the decreasing function
steps.content:
For $g(x)=6(0.75)^x$, the base $0.75$ is between $0$ and $1$. On the domain $x\ge0$, the maximum occurs at $x=0$:
$$
g(0)=6(0.75)^0=6
$$
That maximum is displayed as the coefficient.

steps.title: Select the statement
steps.content: Only Function II displays its maximum value as a coefficient or constant. The answer is B.
